old_claim.note: Programmable Cas9 Nuclease is modeled as a necessary component or method for this technology in the current graph.

new_claim.note: Cas9-compatible single-guide RNA design requires the Cas9 nuclease architecture because the sgRNA scaffold and targeting sequence are engineered to program Cas9 cleavage.

source_shape.source_locator: Abstract and Figure 5 summary describing an engineered single RNA chimera that combines crRNA and tracrRNA features to direct sequence-specific Cas9 DNA cleavage.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The paper reports that a dual tracrRNA:crRNA guide can be engineered as a single RNA chimera and still direct sequence-specific Cas9 double-stranded DNA cleavage.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This licenses a hard architecture edge for the Cas9-scoped sgRNA node because the designed guide RNA is defined by its ability to program Cas9, not merely by historical association with CRISPR.

invariant_preserved_or_changed: Invariant preserved with narrower wording: this node represents Cas9-compatible single-guide RNA design, not all guide-RNA design across every Cas effector family.
would_reject_if: The dependent node were intentionally broadened to all CRISPR guide-RNA design across non-Cas9 effectors.
A source showed the single-guide RNA scaffold described here functions independently of Cas9 or with no Cas9-specific architecture.
The graph removed separate PAM, bioinformatics, or oligonucleotide-synthesis context needed for practical sgRNA target design.
short_reason: For the Cas9-scoped sgRNA node, the 2012 primary paper directly supports Cas9 as a required architecture dependency.
